name: timeline
description: Ordered sequence of events with status markers and a connector line

meta.name: Timeline
meta.description: Ordered sequence of events with status markers and a connector line

props.name: items
props.type: { id: string; title: ReactNode; description?: ReactNode; time?: string; icon?: ReactNode; status?: "complete" | "current" | "upcoming" }[]

examples.title: Vertical timeline with statuses
examples.code:
<Timeline
  items={[
    { id: '1', title: 'Order placed', time: '09:00', status: 'complete' },
    { id: '2', title: 'Shipped', time: '12:30', status: 'current' },
    { id: '3', title: 'Delivered', status: 'upcoming' },
  ]}
/>

whenToUse: Showing an ordered sequence of events over time (activity feeds, order tracking)
Communicating progress through discrete stages with complete/current/upcoming status
Presenting a chronological history where order is meaningful
whenNotToUse: Interactive multi-step navigation the user controls — use Tabs or a Stepper
Unordered lists where sequence carries no meaning — use List

antiPatterns.bad: Marking several items as status="current" at once
antiPatterns.good: Exactly one current item so aria-current="step" points to a single position
antiPatterns.why: Multiple current markers make the active position ambiguous for assistive tech

related.name: List
related.reason: List is for unordered prose; Timeline conveys ordered, time-stamped events

intent.a11yRationale: Rendered as an ordered list (ol/li) to convey sequence; the active item carries aria-current="step" and decorative markers are aria-hidden so screen readers read only the text

flexibility.note: vertical for feeds, horizontal for compact progress strips
Use a single current item; status drives marker colour via data-status
